stem: A phone plan charges $0.10 per text message and $0.25 per minute of calls. A customer has a monthly budget of at most $30 for texts and calls combined. If the customer sends 80 text messages in a month, what is the greatest number of whole minutes of calls the customer can make?
explanation: Text cost: $0.10 \times 80 = $8. Remaining budget for calls: $30 - $8 = $22. Set up $0.25m \leq 22$. Divide by 0.25: $m \leq 88$. The greatest number of whole minutes is 88.

distractorRationale.B: Cluster D error: Ignored the text message cost entirely and solved $0.25m \leq 30$, getting $m \leq 120$.
distractorRationale.C: Cluster B error: Calculated text cost as $0.10 \times 80 = $8$ correctly, but then set up $0.25m \leq 30 - 8 = 22$ and made an arithmetic error dividing $22 \div 0.25$, getting 92 instead of 88 (computing $22 \div 0.24$ by mistake).
distractorRationale.D: Cluster C error: Confused text messages and call minutes, assuming that the number of call minutes must equal or be less than the number of texts, and chose 80.
